title: mentha × rotundifolia var. insularis
intro: Mentha × rotundifolia var. insularis, also known as Corsican mint, is a natural hybrid of the water mint and round-leaved mint plant, characterized by its distinct aroma and unique growth habits. This plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly menthol and other volatile oils, which have been traditionally used to alleviate digestive issues and provide relief from nausea and headaches. The health benefits of Corsican mint include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and essential oils, often employed in aromatherapy and natural skincare products.
